channel.title: Accessibility Review on Mobile A11y
channel.description: Recent content in Accessibility Review on Mobile A11y

item.title: Accessibility Review: Huh? - International languages
item.description:
The Accessibility Review series uses real world apps to provide examples of common accessibility issues and provide tips on how to fix them. Each of the developers has kindly volunteered their app to be tested.

Huh? is a dictionary and thesaurus app from Peter Yaacoub. Enter a word into the search bar then choose a dictionary service. Press search and the app will present your chosen service’s entry for the term you entered.

item.title: Accessibility Review: Figure Case - Button Labels
item.description:
The Accessibility Review series uses real world apps to provide examples of common accessibility issues and provide tips on how to fix them. Each of the developers has kindly volunteered their app to be tested.

Figure Case is an app to help organise a tabletop miniature collection created by Simon Nickel. The app helps to track miniatures you own, and what state they currently find themselves in - unassembled, assembled, or painted.

item.title: Accessibility Review: Daily Dictionary - Screen changes
item.description:
The Accessibility Review series uses real world apps to provide examples of common accessibility issues and provide tips on how to fix them. Each of the developers has kindly volunteered their app to be tested.

Daily Dictionary is an app from Benjamin Mayo providing a new word every day with definitions and real-world uses designed to help increase your vocabulary.

Assessing the app, I noticed Benjamin has made a design decision around presenting the app’s settings. Settings are slid down from the top of the screen, similar to a card UI that you might find in the Apple Maps app. Except with Daily Dictionary, the card is presented from the top of the screen. The card slides down or up in response to the user activating the settings button on the navigation bar.
